perturbation.original_text: Each of the Participants will contribute to the capital of the Joint Venture, in cash or property in agreed upon value. All Participants will contribute their respective Contributions as agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the Participants may contribute to the capital of the Joint Venture, in cash or property, if they deem it necessary. Contributions are optional and not guaranteed by all participants.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that all participants will contribute to the capital of the Joint Venture, creating a mandatory obligation. The modified text uses 'may contribute' and states that contributions are 'optional,' contradicting the original mandatory requirement. This creates uncertainty about the financial obligations of each participant, especially when enforcing the Joint Venture.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to any other provisions of this Agreement, the net profits and losses of the Joint Venture, for both accounting and tax purposes, will accrue to and be borne by the Participants in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Profit and Loss Distribution").
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated capital contributions from all participants, forming the basis for profit and loss distribution as described in the contradicted text: '...will accrue to and be borne by the Participants in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions...'. The changed text makes contributions optional, which directly contradicts the original mandatory requirement, therefore nullifying the Profit and Loss Distribution which is based on capital contributions. This creates a contradiction because if contributions are optional, the basis for profit and loss distribution is undermined.

perturbation.original_text: Any Participant can call a special meeting to resolve issues that require a vote, as indicated by this Agreement, by providing all Participants with reasonable notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Participant can call a special meeting to resolve issues. The Tax Matter Participant should be notified for any meeting.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mentioned that any participant can call for a special meeting and needs to provide all participants with reasonable notice. In the changed text, the rule that everyone needs to be notified has been omitted and only the Tax Matter Participant needs to be notified. This has changed the right of the participants, in comparison with the original version, and creates a contradiction in terms of actions required, creating uncertainty if not everyone is not informed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Regular meetings of the Participants will be held quarterly. Any Participant can call a special meeting to resolve issues that require a vote, as indicated by this Agreement, by providing all Participants with reasonable notice. In the case of a special vote, the meeting will be restricted to the specific purpose for which the meeting was held. All meetings will be held at a time and in a location that is reasonable, convenient and practical considering the situation of all Participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text both state that all participants should be notified for a special meeting. The changed text states that only the Tax Matter Participant should be notified for a special meeting. This directly contradicts the requirement to notify *all* participants and creates uncertainty regarding who needs to be notified, thus creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where conflicting implications are introduced by a modification.

perturbation.original_text: Written notice of intention to withdraw must be served in writing upon the remaining Participants at least Thirty (30) business days prior to the withdrawal date. The voluntary withdrawal of a Participant will result in the dissolution of the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: Written notice of intention to withdraw must be served in writing upon the remaining Participants at least Thirty (30) business days prior to the withdrawal date. The voluntary withdrawal of a Participant may result in the dissolution of the Joint Venture.
perturbation.explanation: The original text definitively stated that voluntary withdrawal *will* result in dissolution, creating certainty. The modified text uses 'may result', introducing uncertainty about whether withdrawal leads to dissolution, as it is now conditional. This directly contradicts the original binding statement, making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the Joint Venture may be dissolved only with the unanimous consent of all Participants.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the outcome of a Participant's voluntary withdrawal from a certain 'will result in dissolution' to an uncertain 'may result in dissolution'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Joint Venture may be dissolved 'only with the unanimous consent of all Participants'. The original text established a specific scenario (voluntary withdrawal) that would lead to dissolution, but the changed text introduces ambiguity. This now conflicts with the original condition requiring unanimous consent for dissolution, creating a strong in-text contradiction and falling under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the 'clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications'.
